mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate, lowering the present value of equity cash flows, and is associated with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level vs 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any rolling 2-year window, or if the mean return when FEDFUNDS < 2% is not significantly greater than zero at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals labor market slack and declining consumer income expectations, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If within 12 months of UNRATE crossing above 6%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative, or if the false positive rate exceeds 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer and investment demand, which drives corporate earnings upward and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint signal amplifies when both are above their respective 6-month moving averages.
failure_condition: If the composite signal of both proxies rising does not produce a mean 20-day return significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05), or if the interaction term in a regression is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a threshold of 3% year-over-year, the central bank is forced into a tightening stance, raising short-term rates and compressing equity valuations. This inflationary regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after CPIAUCSL yoy crosses above 3%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative or the hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity, which boosts real economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive at 10% level over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerating above a 3% annualized rate signals an overheating demand environment that eventually forces the Fed to tighten, but in the near term it boosts sentiment and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This sketch uses the initial acceleration as threshold trigger.
failure_condition: If within 6 months of GDP growth crossing above 3%, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not positive, or if the strategy exhibits a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 when triggered.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payroll employment and a decline in the unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market with rising wage pressure. Initially, this supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the composite indicator (PAYEMS rising month-over-month and UNRATE falling month-over-month) does not predict positive forward returns with a hit rate above 55% over a rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime (defined as when real retail sales have crossed above their pre-recession peak), further increases in retail sales signal sustained consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If during the recovery regime, the average 5-day S&P 500 return following positive RSAFS monthly changes is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), or if the signal is not robust to the choice of pre-recession peak definition.
